examples.title: Vulnerable Login Query
examples.description: Demonstrates unsafe SQL query construction vulnerable to injection
examples.code:
# VULNERABLE CODE - DO NOT USE
username = request.form['username']
password = request.form['password']

# Directly concatenating user input into query
query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '{username}' AND password = '{password}'"
cursor.execute(query)

# Attacker input: username = "admin' --" bypasses authentication
examples.explanation: This code is vulnerable because user input is directly concatenated into SQL query. Attacker can inject SQL code through input fields to manipulate query logic.

examples.title: Secure Parameterized Query
examples.description: Shows secure way to write SQL queries using parameterized statements
examples.code:
# SECURE CODE - Using Parameterized Queries
username = request.form['username']
password = request.form['password']

# Using parameterized query with placeholders
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = %s AND password = %s"
cursor.execute(query, (username, password))

# User input is now treated as data, not executable code
examples.explanation: Parameterized queries use placeholders (%s) and pass user input separately. The database treats all input as data, preventing SQL injection.

examples.title: Blind SQL Injection Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how blind SQLi extracts information through true/false responses
examples.code:
# Simulating Blind SQL Injection Attack
# Attacker tests each character of password

# Example: Extract password character by character
for i in range(1, 33):
    for ascii_val in range(32, 127):
        char = chr(ascii_val)
        payload = f"' AND SUBSTRING(password, {i}, 1) = '{char}' --"
        
        # If response is normal (login fails), guess was wrong
        # If response shows user exists, guess was correct
        # Continue until entire password is reconstructed

# Time-based blind SQLi: If true, database sleeps for 5 seconds
payload = "' AND IF(1=1, SLEEP(5), 0) --"
examples.explanation: Blind SQLi doesn't see errors or data. Attackers ask true/false questions and infer information from application responses (normal vs error, time delays).

examples.title: Secure Prepared Statement (Java)
examples.description: Shows secure database query using Java PreparedStatement
examples.explanation: PreparedStatement compiles the query structure first, then binds user input as parameters. The input cannot alter the query structure, preventing SQL injection.
